what_it_does: A source-grounded research finding that analyzes vLLM 0.21's V1 scheduler, priority scheduling, chunked prefill, KV-offload, prefix caching, and preemption behavior to determine whether one loaded model instance can serve both deep-context conversation and many-small worker workloads, and it maps the exact company-stack wiring changes needed to make that work.
observations: vLLM 0.21 has a real priority scheduler (policy='priority'), but the company's OpenAI transport allowlist does not copy the request-level 'priority' field, so the feature is inert today.
Priority is a top-level request field, not a SamplingParams field, and lower numeric values are handled earlier with ties broken by arrival time.
The 0.21 PriorityRequestQueue has no aging or anti-starvation mechanism; starvation avoidance depends on intermittent chat workload shape.
Chunked prefill is on by default for the served model and batches decodes before prefills, letting a deep prefill interleave with many small decodes.
max_num_seqs auto-derives to 256 on a <70 GiB card, which reserves roughly 8.5 GiB of per-sequence Mamba state and would OOM; it must stay explicit and loadout-sourced.
Native KV-offload handles both AttentionSpec and MambaSpec layers, so the full hybrid conversation state (attention KV plus GatedDeltaNet/SSM state) can spill to CPU RAM and reload without recompute.
Prefix caching is on by default but the 1056-token block/page size makes it mostly ineffective for short small-worker prompts while still valuable for long conversation prefixes.

One instance at max_num_seqs=32 with priority, chunked prefill, and KV-offload can serve both deep and swarm shapes; the remaining reason to keep loadout distinctions is co-residency of embedder/voice, not workload shape.
Default preemption is RECOMPUTE: preempted requests are requeued with num_computed_tokens=0, and under priority the lowest-priority running request is preempted first.
Async scheduling is opt-in and proposed as adopt-and-verify for the worker/solo loadout.

The serve-flags resolver should gain capability rows for scheduling-policy, max-num-batched-tokens, kv-offloading-size, and async-scheduling, while max-num-seqs stays sourced from the loadout's declared concurrency.
standouts: Priority scheduling is real in vLLM but a dead wire in the company stack because the transport allowlist omits the field.
Native KV-offload moves both attention KV and Mamba/SSM state, enabling whole-hybrid-conversation persistence across eviction.
The two-loadout split for conversational-vs-worker shape tension largely dissolves; co-residency is the surviving axis.
max_num_seqs must remain explicit because vLLM's auto-derived default is unsafe for this hybrid architecture.
conventions: Uses research-finding frontmatter with type, register, area, title, status, coverage, and tags.
Tags evidence as Observed, Inferred, External, or My-idea.
Includes a contradiction/upgrade table comparing findings against the anchor/SCOPE.
Explicitly marks live-behavior claims as needs-verification because the brain was not reconfigured during writing.
Interleaves measured numbers, source quotes, and design proposals in one narrative.
concerns: Several strong conclusions rely on unverified live behavior, especially WSL2 KV-offload throughput and async-scheduling stability.
The proposed single-point priority wiring assumes all generation paths funnel through run_role or the same transport; the file does not exhaustively prove no bypass exists.
My-idea design recommendations are mixed with source observations, which could blur evidence and opinion.
Numerical budget arguments use approximate values and margins without sensitivity analysis.
notes: A detailed scheduler/cache deep-read that concludes one vLLM instance can handle both deep and swarm workloads, but only after wiring priority through the transport and keeping max_num_seqs explicit.
questions: Which exact services.json loadout schema fields will carry scheduling, concurrency, kv_offload_gib, and async flags?
How will the transport decide whether to send priority=0 or omit the field when the active loadout uses fcfs scheduling?
What is the measured WSL2 pageable-transfer latency for MambaSpec KV-offload on this model?
Does async scheduling actually improve ITL for the 32-way swarm without instability?
Are there any direct client.complete paths outside run_role that also need priority wiring?
How much does 1056-block prefix-cache fragmentation reduce effective shelf capacity for the 32-way worker in practice?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose—assess whether one loaded model can serve mixed workloads via scheduler/cache features—matches the file's detailed analysis, config mapping, and build-wiring proposal.
apparent_themes: Simplifying loadout complexity by leveraging built-in vLLM features
Co-residency as the real loadout axis
intention_signals: The file is trying to collapse the conversational/worker two-loadout split into a single vLLM instance by proving the scheduler and cache built-ins can time-share the two shapes, while honestly cataloguing the code changes and caveats.
novelty: A deep read of vLLM 0.21 V1 scheduler and native KV-offload worker specifically for a hybrid Mamba/attention model, paired with a concrete company-stack wiring proposal for the transport allowlist and serve-flags resolver.
contribution: A source-grounded analysis of vLLM scheduler, priority, chunked prefill, KV-offload, and prefix-cache behavior, plus a concrete mapping of where to inject scheduling, priority passthrough, KV-offload, and async-scheduling configuration into the company's serve-flags resolver and transport layer.
summary_for_embedding: A research finding on vLLM 0.21 scheduler, concurrency, and cache features that argues one model instance can serve both deep-context and many-small workloads and details the company-stack wiring required to enable priority scheduling, KV-offload, and related flags.
intention_for_embedding: To determine whether the GPU-serving rework can drop the conversational-vs-worker loadout split by leveraging vLLM V1 scheduler features and to specify the exact transport and serve-config changes needed.
